rulings.Case Title: JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION VS. SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE OF THE MARY L. ELLIS 2004 TRUST ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 9, 2018 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATIO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend to allege facts in support of each element of this cause of action as to the moving defendant under Cal. Welf. & Inst. Code 15610.30 and/or 15657.6.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JESSIE STREET COLLECTIVE, LLC VS. STANLEY CHIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defts Stanley Chia And Elizabeth Wu'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 9, 2018 line 2. Defendants Stanley Chia And Elizabeth Wu'S Demurrer To Complaint OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Each cause of action is sufficiently alleged. The option provision provides an ascertainable standard for determination of rent for the option period. Etco Corp. v. Hauer (1984) 161 Cal.app.3d 1154. Causes of action for premises liability and negligence are sufficiently alleged. Defendants' argument regarding the types of damages available under these causes of action to Plaintiff raised for the first time in the reply cannot be properly considered. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: RANDOLPH R. SCOTT ET AL VS. ZIEVE, BRODNAX & STEELE LLP ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 9, 2018 line 5. DEFENDANT ZIEVE, BRODNAX & STEELE LLP's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED in part and moot in part. Demurrer is moot as to causes of action for punitive damages and costs. See ruling on Motion to Strike. Demurrer to causes of action for statutory violation and for conversion is overruled. Complaint sufficiently alleges an overcharge, whether the overcharge is based on the "capped" amount or on reasonableness of the actual fees and costs incurred in the pre and post foreclosure stages is an issue of fact not properly addressed on demurrer; additionally whether particular fees and costs are subject to "cap" is also an issue of fact. See Caruso v. Great Western Savings (1991) 229 Cal.App.3d 667. ("Whether a particular expense may be treated as an advance, or is subject to the limitations in Civil Code section 2924c, will depend upon the purpose for which the expense was incurred and the particular contractual terms involved." (212 Cal.App.3d at p. 421, italics added.) = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: RANDOLPH R. SCOTT ET AL VS. ZIEVE, BRODNAX & STEELE LLP ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 9, 2018 line 5. DEFENDANT ZIEVE, BRODNAX & STEELE LLP's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to re-allege causes of action for punitive damages and costs as claims for relief within other causes of action (if proper) and to allege facts in support of request for punitive damages. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 10 HAWTHORNE STREET, LLC VS. JOHN BERGGRUEN GALLERY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Defendants 10 Hawthorne Street, Llc, Richard Tavernetti And Maur Tavernetti'S Notice Of Special Motion And Special Motion To Strike 5th Cause Of Action And Related Allegations (Slapp)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 9, 2018 line 6. CROSS DEFENDANT 10 HAWTHORNE STREET, LLC, RICHARD TAVERNETTI, MAUR TAVERNETTI'S Special Motion To Strike 5th Cause Of Action And Related Allegations (Slapp) GRANTED only as to Cross-Claimant's Fifth Cause of Action. Cross-Claimant's defamation claim is based on a 3-Day Notice to Quit, which is (1) protected constitutional activity and (2) has no way of prevailing under CCP § 47(b). Additionally, Cross-Claimant is given leave to amend to include a malicious prosecution cause of action. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: LAN LE VS. ZANZI KNIGHT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 9, 2018 line 7. DEFENDANTs ZANZI KNIGHT, SUNDIATA KNIGHT, and THABITI KNIGHT's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to Vacate judgment is DENIED. Defendant fails to sustain his burden under CCP 473(b) and show that December 14, 2017 judgment was entered due to excusable neglect, surprise or mistake. Motion is GRANTED to the extent is seeks to recall the writ issued on December 15, 2017. December 14, 2017 judgment was entered based on breach of stipulation which required a payment of $1,772. This amount was timely remitted (per judgment) on December 14, 2017. Section 8 portion of the rental payment has not been before the court on the motion to enforce judgment and has not been required under the judgment. However, it appears that Defendant failed to comply with the second requirement under the judgment, i.e. abate the violation and file DPH report memorializing abatement by January 3, 2018. Plaintiff may obtain a new writ forthwith. Plaintiff to prepare order in conformity with this ruling. = (501/REQ)
